title: asperula glomerata subsp. condensata
intro: Asperula glomerata subsp. condensata, also known as the Clustered Woodruff, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This perennial herb has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its medicinal properties include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Clustered Woodruff include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Clustered Woodruff can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies and holistic health practices.
